name: Hunter Long
headline: Efficient backup tight end stuck behind Strange in Jacksonville
body:
Long's 2025 advanced profile is a tale of efficiency wasted on volume. Across seven games he posted a strong 0.457 EPA per play and a 58.8% success rate, and his catchable-target rate held up enough to score two touchdowns on just 17 looks. But the underlying usage is microscopic: a 23.1% route share, 3.1% target share, and a 0.056 WOPR all scream backup. A 23.5% drop rate on four total drops further caps trust, and at 39.9% snaps he never controlled the formation.

The path forward stays narrow. Long sits behind Brenton Strange on Jacksonville's depth chart and profiles as the next-man-up only if Strange misses time. Projections of roughly five catches, 44 yards and one score for the full season confirm there's no standalone value here. Hip and knee issues have already clouded his availability. He's a desperation streamer at best until injury opens real snaps.
